supports_families: Delay & escalation (costly delay, war of attrition, dollar-auction entrapment)
payload.title: Perfect Equilibrium in a Bargaining Model
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model: unique subgame-perfect equilibrium of infinite-horizon alternating-offers bargaining with discounting; first-mover share x* = (1-delta_b)/(1-delta_a*delta_b). No estimation - identity from discount factors.

payload.authors: Guth, W.; Schmittberger, R.; Schwarze, B.
payload.title: An experimental analysis of ultimatum bargaining
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate (lab): founding ultimatum-game experiment; proposers offer far above the subgame-perfect ~0 split (modal offers near equal split), and low offers are rejected - the anomaly that motivates behavioral corrections to Rubinstein.

payload.authors: Oosterbeek, H.; Sloof, R.; van de Kuilen, G.
payload.title: Cultural Differences in Ultimatum Game Experiments: Evidence from a Meta-Analysis
payload.what_it_provides: meta_analysis: 37 papers / 75 results. Pooled mean proposer offer = 40% of pie; mean rejection rate = 16% of offers. Offer falls with pie size; rejection falls with offered share. Responder (not proposer) behavior differs across regions.

payload.title: Behavioral Game Theory: Experiments in Strategic Interaction
payload.what_it_provides: descriptive/meta synthesis: modal and median ultimatum offers are 40-50% of the stake; offers below ~20% of the pie are rejected roughly half the time. The best-replicated behavioral regularities in the family.

payload.authors: Faratin, P.; Sierra, C.; Jennings, N. R.
payload.title: Negotiation Decision Functions for Autonomous Agents
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model (agent-design heuristic): parameterized families of time-dependent (polynomial/exponential, Boulware vs Conceder), resource-dependent, and behavior-dependent (tit-for-tat) concession tactics. Defines the functional form used in the registry; parameters are engineering choices, NOT estimated from human data.

supports_families: Delay & escalation (costly delay, war of attrition, dollar-auction entrapment)
payload.title: The theory of games and the evolution of animal conflicts
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model: the war of attrition. Two contestants pay a cost proportional to time spent persisting for a prize of value V; the symmetric ESS is a mixed strategy in which giving-up times are exponentially distributed with rate 1/V, and expected payoff is dissipated to zero. Canonical model of costly-delay standoffs.

supports_families: Delay & escalation (costly delay, war of attrition, dollar-auction entrapment)
payload.title: The Dollar Auction game: a paradox in noncooperative behavior and escalation
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model: all-pay escalation trap. A prize is auctioned but the top TWO bidders both pay their last bid; rational players are drawn into bidding past the prize's value (sunk-cost entrapment). Canonical model of conflict escalation / commitment traps.

payload.title: The Theory of Political Coalitions
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model + descriptive claim: the 'size principle' - in n-person zero-sum games with side-payments, rational players form MINIMAL winning coalitions (just large enough to win, no larger). Basis for the greedy-MWC operator.

payload.title: Weighted Voting Doesn't Work: A Mathematical Analysis
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model (exact index): the Banzhaf power index = fraction of swing/critical coalitions in which a member is pivotal. No parameters to fit - a deterministic function of weights and quota. Directly implemented (banzhaf_power).

payload.title: A Method for Evaluating the Distribution of Power in a Committee System
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model (exact index): Shapley-Shubik power index = probability a member is pivotal over all orderings (Shapley value of the simple voting game). Complementary exact power measure; no fitting.

supports_families: Coalition stability & defection (core/nucleolus, Gamson's law payoffs, legislative defection)
payload.title: A Theory of Coalition Formation
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model + associational law: the parity/proportionality norm - a member expects a share of the coalition payoff proportional to the resources it contributes. Origin of 'Gamson's law' for portfolio allocation and a stability condition (deviations invite defection).

payload.authors: Browne, E. C.; Franklin, M. N.
supports_families: Coalition stability & defection (core/nucleolus, Gamson's law payoffs, legislative defection)
payload.title: Aspects of Coalition Payoffs in European Parliamentary Democracies
payload.what_it_provides: associational (classic empirical test): across 13 postwar European democracies, cabinet-portfolio share is strongly proportional to a party's seat contribution; systematic small-party overpayment / large-party underpayment at the extremes.

payload.authors: Warwick, P. V.; Druckman, J. N.
supports_families: Coalition stability & defection (core/nucleolus, Gamson's law payoffs, legislative defection)
payload.title: The portfolio allocation paradox: An investigation into the nature of a very strong but puzzling relationship
payload.what_it_provides: associational (refinement/replication): confirms near-1:1 portfolio-to-seat proportionality (very high R^2); regression through the origin yields a slope close to one, with a small-party bonus captured by a positive intercept in the free-intercept specification.

supports_families: Coalition stability & defection (core/nucleolus, Gamson's law payoffs, legislative defection)
payload.title: The Nucleolus of a Characteristic Function Game
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model: the nucleolus - a unique, always-existing stable payoff allocation that lexicographically minimizes the maximum coalition 'excess' (dissatisfaction). With the CORE, provides the formal stability/defection-proofness criterion for coalition payoffs.

supports_families: Coalition stability & defection (core/nucleolus, Gamson's law payoffs, legislative defection)
payload.title: Estimating Party Influence in Congressional Roll-Call Voting
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate: a two-stage method separating members' preferences from party pressure; detects significant majority-party influence on roll-call voting (concentrated on party-dividing / agenda votes) - the causal counterpart of legislative-coalition defection/discipline.

payload.title: Experience-weighted Attraction Learning in Normal Form Games
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.authors: Teck-Hua Ho; Colin Camerer; Juin-Kuan Chong
payload.title: Self-tuning experience weighted attraction learning in games
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.authors: Richard D. McKelvey; Thomas R. Palfrey
payload.title: Quantal Response Equilibria for Normal Form Games
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.authors: Jacob K. Goeree; Charles A. Holt; Thomas R. Palfrey
payload.title: Quantal Response Equilibrium: A Stochastic Theory of Games
payload.what_it_provides: methodological

payload.title: Predicting How People Play Games: Reinforcement Learning in Experimental Games with Unique, Mixed Strategy Equilibria
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.title: Learning in Extensive-Form Games: Experimental Data and Simple Dynamic Models in the Intermediate Term
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.authors: Christopher J. C. H. Watkins; Peter Dayan
payload.title: Q-learning
payload.what_it_provides: methodological

payload.title: Individual Learning in Normal Form Games: Some Laboratory Results
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.title: The Theory of Learning in Games
payload.venue: MIT Press (companion survey: 'Learning in games', European Economic Review 42:631-639)
payload.what_it_provides: methodological

payload.authors: Phillippa Lally; Cornelia H. M. van Jaarsveld; Henry W. W. Potts; Jane Wardle
payload.title: How are habits formed: Modelling habit formation in the real world
payload.what_it_provides: descriptive

payload.title: A new look at habits and the habit-goal interface
payload.what_it_provides: methodological

payload.title: Reflections on past behavior: A self-report index of habit strength (SRHI)
payload.what_it_provides: methodological

payload.title: A Behavioral Model of Rational Choice
payload.what_it_provides: methodological

payload.authors: Andrew Caplin; Mark Dean; Daniel Martin
payload.title: Search and Satisficing
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.authors: Erin L. Krupka; Roberto A. Weber
payload.title: Identifying Social Norms Using Coordination Games: Why Does Dictator Game Sharing Vary?
payload.venue: Journal of the European Economic Association 11(3):495-524
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model

payload.title: The Grammar of Society: The Nature and Dynamics of Social Norms
payload.what_it_provides: methodological

payload.authors: Goldenberg, J.; Libai, B.; Muller, E.
payload.title: Talk of the Network: A Complex Systems Look at the Underlying Process of Word-of-Mouth
payload.what_it_provides: Independent-cascade / stochastic-cellular-automaton word-of-mouth formalism: each active tie transmits with a per-contact probability independently — the structural basis for the linear-in-exposure hazard lambda=q*k.

payload.authors: De Domenico, M.; Lima, A.; Mougel, P.; Musolesi, M.
payload.title: The Anatomy of a Scientific Rumor
payload.what_it_provides: Source of the local Higgs Twitter diffusion dataset (~500k users, 1-7 July 2012; retweet/reply/mention/follower networks) on which the simple-contagion hazard was fitted as a baseline (SNAP higgs-twitter).

payload.title: The Spread of Behavior in an Online Social Network Experiment
payload.what_it_provides: Randomized controlled experiment: behavior (joining a health forum) spread FARTHER and FASTER on clustered-lattice networks than on matched random networks, and individual adoption was much more likely when a participant received social reinforcement from MULTIPLE neighbors — the causal anchor for reinforcement-dependent (complex) contagion.

payload.title: Complex Contagions and the Weakness of Long Ties
payload.what_it_provides: Formal theory distinguishing simple vs complex contagion; when adoption needs multiple independent confirmations, wide/long bridging ties become LESS effective and local clustering (redundant ties) becomes advantageous — the theoretical basis for the superlinear/threshold hazard shape.

payload.title: Threshold Models of Collective Behavior
payload.what_it_provides: Defines an individual threshold as the proportion of the group one must see adopt before joining; shows aggregate outcomes are a knife-edge function of the threshold DISTRIBUTION (the 100-person riot example) — foundation of fraction-of-neighbors adoption rules.

payload.title: A Simple Model of Global Cascades on Random Networks
payload.what_it_provides: Networked threshold model + a cascade condition: global cascades occur when a percolating 'vulnerable cluster' exists; cascades are rare and occur in a bounded connectivity window, with bimodal cascade sizes at high connectivity.

payload.authors: Romero, D. M.; Meeder, B.; Kleinberg, J.
payload.title: Differences in the Mechanics of Information Diffusion Across Topics: Idioms, Political Hashtags, and Complex Contagion on Twitter
payload.venue: Proc. 20th International World Wide Web Conference (WWW '11), pp. 695-704
payload.what_it_provides: Defines STICKINESS (probability of adoption given one-or-more exposures) and PERSISTENCE (relative extent to which REPEATED exposures keep having large marginal effect). Empirical finding: politically controversial hashtags are the MOST PERSISTENT — repeated exposures keep raising adoption (a complex-contagion signature) — whereas idioms are sticky but plateau. First large-scale observational validation that content types differ in complex-contagion behavior.

payload.title: A New Product Growth for Model Consumer Durables
payload.what_it_provides: The Bass diffusion model: hazard of first purchase = p + q*F(t), with p = coefficient of innovation (external influence) and q = coefficient of imitation (internal word-of-mouth), over a fixed finite population m; tested on 11 consumer durables. Closed-form S-curve adoption with saturation at m.

payload.authors: Sultan, F.; Farley, J. U.; Lehmann, D. R.
payload.title: A Meta-Analysis of Applications of Diffusion Models
payload.what_it_provides: Meta-analysis of ~213 diffusion-model applications: mean coefficient of innovation p ~ 0.03 (SD ~0.03) and mean coefficient of imitation q ~ 0.38 (SD ~0.35) — the canonical published-research prior ranges for Bass p and q.

payload.title: Spectra of Some Self-Exciting and Mutually Exciting Point Processes
payload.what_it_provides: Original self-exciting point process: conditional intensity lambda(t) = mu + integral of a kernel over past events, so each event raises the rate of future events — the structural basis of self-excitation.

payload.authors: Zhao, Q.; Erdogdu, M. A.; He, H. Y.; Rajaraman, A.; Leskovec, J.
payload.title: SEISMIC: A Self-Exciting Point Process Model for Predicting Tweet Popularity
payload.what_it_provides: Where Hawkes WORKS: predicting the FINAL retweet/reshare count of a single cascade from its early timestamps + node degrees; intensity = infectivity x accumulated self-excitation. Cascade-SIZE prediction, not aggregate multi-cascade count forecasting.

payload.authors: Mishra, S.; Rizoiu, M.-A.; Xie, L.
payload.title: Feature Driven and Point Process Approaches for Popularity Prediction
payload.what_it_provides: Marked Hawkes for cascade popularity: estimates content virality, memory decay, and user influence; hybrid with features. Confirms the regime where Hawkes adds value = per-cascade total-popularity prediction.

payload.title: Novelty and Collective Attention
payload.what_it_provides: Empirical measurement (1M Digg users): collective attention to a novel item decays with a STRETCHED-EXPONENTIAL law governed by a single novelty factor, with a characteristic decay time of approximately ONE HOUR on Digg news — establishes that novelty/attention has an intrinsic fading time scale.

payload.title: TiDeH: Time-Dependent Hawkes Process for Predicting Retweet Dynamics
payload.venue: Proc. 10th International AAAI Conference on Web and Social Media (ICWSM 2016)
payload.what_it_provides: Operationalizes information aging inside a point process: a time-dependent Hawkes intensity with an explicit aging (memory-decay) term plus circadian modulation, fitted to retweet dynamics (parameters p0, r0, phi0, tau_m) — a methodological template for embedding a decay time constant in a diffusion hazard.

payload.title: The Strength of Weak Ties
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model/theory: foundational claim that weak ties bridge otherwise-disconnected clusters and carry non-redundant information (e.g. job leads); qualitative, non-causal.

payload.authors: Karthik Rajkumar, Guillaume Saint-Jacques, Iavor Bojinov, Erik Brynjolfsson, Sinan Aral
payload.title: A causal test of the strength of weak ties
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate: randomized LinkedIn People-You-May-Know experiments varying weak-tie prevalence for >20M users over 5 years (~2B new ties, ~600k new jobs); establishes a CAUSAL, nonlinear (inverted-U) effect of tie strength on job mobility.

payload.authors: Miller McPherson, Lynn Smith-Lovin, James M. Cook
payload.title: Birds of a Feather: Homophily in Social Networks
payload.what_it_provides: descriptive/review: documents that homophily pervasively structures ties of every type — the reason tied nodes co-behave even absent influence.

payload.authors: Sinan Aral, Lev Muchnik, Arun Sundararajan
payload.title: Distinguishing influence-based contagion from homophily-driven diffusion in dynamic networks
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate (bounded): dynamic matched-sample estimation on a large IM/mobile-service adoption network shows homophily explains >=50% of apparent contagion and naive methods overstate influence by 300-700% — an UPPER BOUND on influence, not point identification.

payload.authors: Cosma Rohilla Shalizi, Andrew C. Thomas
payload.title: Homophily and Contagion Are Generically Confounded in Observational Social Network Studies
payload.what_it_provides: methodological: proves homophily, contagion, and covariate causation are GENERICALLY CONFOUNDED in observational network data; separation requires randomization or strong, unverifiable parametric/covariate assumptions.

payload.authors: David A. Kim, Alison R. Hwong, Derek Stafford, D. Alex Hughes, A. James O'Malley, James H. Fowler, Nicholas A. Christakis
supports_families: Network targeting / seeding (friendship-nomination targeting; friendship paradox)
payload.title: Social network targeting to maximise population behaviour change: a cluster randomised controlled trial
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate: cluster RCT (32 rural-Honduras villages, 5,773 participants; NCT01672580) randomizing seed-TARGETING method; friendship-nomination seeding causally beat random and in-degree seeding for health-behavior adoption.

supports_families: Network targeting / seeding (friendship-nomination targeting; friendship paradox)
payload.title: Why Your Friends Have More Friends Than You Do
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model: the friendship paradox — a randomly nominated friend has, in expectation, higher degree than a randomly chosen individual; the mathematical basis for why nomination targeting reaches more-central seeds without measuring the network.

payload.authors: Tom A. B. Snijders, Gerhard G. van de Bunt, Christian E. G. Steglich
supports_families: Network rewiring / edge formation & decay (SAOM; triadic closure)
payload.title: Introduction to stochastic actor-based models for network dynamics
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model/methodological: canonical SAOM — continuous-time Markov process where actors myopically optimize a network objective (density, reciprocity, transitive triads, homophily) via tie micro-steps; parameters estimated from network panel waves (RSiena).

supports_families: Network rewiring / edge formation & decay (SAOM; triadic closure)
payload.title: Empirical Analysis of an Evolving Social Network
payload.what_it_provides: associational/descriptive: on a university EMAIL network (43,553 people, 1 year) tie formation is driven by closure of short cycles (triadic closure) plus shared foci/attribute similarity — directly analogous to the Enron temporal email data.

payload.authors: Mehrdad Farajtabar, Yichen Wang, Manuel Gomez-Rodriguez, Shuang Li, Hongyuan Zha, Le Song
payload.title: COEVOLVE: A Joint Point Process Model for Information Diffusion and Network Co-evolution
payload.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 28 (NeurIPS 2015); extended in JMLR 2017
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model: coupled temporal point processes where information-diffusion intensity is modulated by the network and link-creation intensity is modulated by diffusion (mutual excitation); efficient interleaved simulation; code at github.com/Networks-Learning/Coevolution.

supports_families: DeGroot naive learning (opinion averaging on a network)
payload.title: Reaching a Consensus
payload.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association 69(345):118-121
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model — the canonical linear repeated-averaging consensus operator o_i(t+1)=Σ_j w_ij o_j(t) with row-stochastic W and its convergence/consensus conditions.

supports_families: DeGroot naive learning (opinion averaging on a network)
payload.title: Naïve Learning in Social Networks and the Wisdom of Crowds
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model — asymptotic-learning ('wisdom of crowds') theorem for DeGroot updating: beliefs converge to truth iff the eigenvector-centrality influence of the most influential agent vanishes as n grows. NOTE: task listed this as 'QJE'; the actual venue is AEJ: Microeconomics — the Golub-Jackson QJE paper is the 2012 homophily paper (10.1093/qje/qjs021), a different article.

payload.authors: Arun G. Chandrasekhar; Horacio Larreguy; Juan Pablo Xandri
supports_families: DeGroot naive learning (opinion averaging on a network)
Bayesian social learning / observational learning & information cascades
payload.title: Testing Models of Social Learning on Networks: Evidence From Two Experiments
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate / structural — lab-in-the-field + student-lab test estimating a Bayesian/DeGroot type mixture; central result: the simple DeGroot averaging model better explains subject behavior than the Bayesian model, especially as the learning task gets harder and in the field (village) sample. Real evidence that people are NOT literal Bayesian updaters and only partially DeGroot.

payload.title: Opinion Dynamics and Bounded Confidence: Models, Analysis and Simulation
payload.venue: Journal of Artificial Societies and Social Simulation (JASSS) 5(3), article 2
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model (agent-based) — the HK simultaneous-averaging rule: each agent moves to the mean of all opinions within confidence bound epsilon; analysis of when this yields consensus, polarization, or fragmentation. JASSS uses article numbering (no DOI).

payload.authors: Guillaume Deffuant; David Neau; Frédéric Amblard; Gérard Weisbuch
payload.title: Mixing Beliefs among Interacting Agents
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model (agent-based) — the pairwise Deffuant-Weisbuch variant: on a random encounter, two agents partially converge (rate mu) iff their opinion gap is below epsilon; high epsilon -> single cluster, low epsilon -> multiple clusters.

payload.authors: Sushil Bikhchandani; David Hirshleifer; Ivo Welch
supports_families: Bayesian social learning / observational learning & information cascades
payload.title: A Theory of Fads, Fashion, Custom, and Cultural Change as Informational Cascades
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model — the foundational informational-cascade model: rational agents observing predecessors' actions (not their signals) optimally ignore their own private signal once a public belief locks, so information stops aggregating (fragile herding).

payload.authors: Lisa R. Anderson; Charles A. Holt
supports_families: Bayesian social learning / observational learning & information cascades
payload.title: Information Cascades in the Laboratory
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate (lab) — canonical experimental confirmation that rational information cascades form in an urn-signal design roughly as Bayesian theory predicts, including incorrect cascades.

supports_families: Latent vs expressed opinion / preference falsification
payload.title: Private Truths, Public Lies: The Social Consequences of Preference Falsification
payload.what_it_provides: structural_model (theory) — the preference-falsification framework: expressed opinion diverges from private opinion under social pressure; availability cascades and the resulting unpredictability of preference reversals (e.g., sudden revolutions).

payload.authors: Abir De; Isabel Valera; Niloy Ganguly; Sourangshu Bhattacharya; Manuel Gomez-Rodriguez
supports_families: Latent vs expressed opinion / preference falsification
payload.title: Learning and Forecasting Opinion Dynamics in Social Networks (SLANT)
payload.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 29 (NeurIPS 2016)
payload.what_it_provides: methodological / structural — SLANT: a latent-opinion model where each user's hidden opinion follows a marked jump-diffusion SDE driven by neighbors' expressed messages (a marked temporal point process); gives estimable parameters from timestamped event data and forecasting formulas. A concrete latent-vs-expressed estimation template.

supports_families: Interpersonal persuasion / durable attitude change (with backfire & minimal-effects nulls)
payload.title: Durably reducing transphobia: A field experiment on door-to-door canvassing
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate (RCT) — strongest causal source: a single ~10-minute perspective-taking canvassing conversation produced a reduction in anti-transgender prejudice that persisted at least 3 months; n=501 voters, 56 canvassers; effect exceeded the 1998-2012 US average decline in homophobia. Demonstrates durable interpersonal persuasion is possible.

payload.authors: Joshua L. Kalla; David E. Broockman
supports_families: Interpersonal persuasion / durable attitude change (with backfire & minimal-effects nulls)
Political persuasion (campaign contact on vote choice)
payload.title: The Minimal Persuasive Effects of Campaign Contact in General Elections: Evidence from 49 Field Experiments
payload.what_it_provides: meta_analysis — best estimate of the persuasive effect of campaign contact/advertising on candidate choice in US general elections is approximately ZERO across 49 field experiments; non-trivial persuasion appears only in rare circumstances (very unpopular positions + heavy persuadable-targeting, or early contact that then decays). The key null / upper-bound on generic persuasion.

supports_families: Interpersonal persuasion / durable attitude change (with backfire & minimal-effects nulls)
payload.title: The Elusive Backfire Effect: Mass Attitudes' Steadfast Factual Adherence
payload.what_it_provides: causal_estimate / large-N experimental — across thousands of subjects and dozens of issues, factual corrections generally move beliefs toward the facts; the 'backfire effect' is rare, not a general phenomenon. Bounds the continued-influence/backfire failure mode.

payload.authors: William H. Riker; Peter C. Ordeshook
payload.title: A Theory of the Calculus of Voting
payload.what_it_provides: Formal expected-utility model of the decision to vote (structural). Introduces the D term (citizen duty) to rescue rationality of voting.

payload.title: What Affects Voter Turnout? (review)
payload.what_it_provides: Review of empirical turnout determinants and the descriptive-fit limits of rational-choice/calculus-of-voting (the 'paradox of voting'). DOI/venue not re-fetched; cite as review context only.

payload.authors: Alan S. Gerber; Donald P. Green
payload.title: The Effects of Canvassing, Telephone Calls, and Direct Mail on Voter Turnout: A Field Experiment
payload.what_it_provides: Foundational randomized GOTV field experiment (New Haven 1998, ~30,000 voters): face-to-face canvassing raises turnout substantially, direct mail slightly, commercial phone calls not at all.

payload.authors: Donald P. Green; Alan S. Gerber
payload.title: Get Out the Vote: How to Increase Voter Turnout (book, meta-estimates)
payload.what_it_provides: Meta-synthesis rules of thumb across hundreds of GOTV experiments (canvassing ~1 added vote per ~14 contacts; commercial phone ~0; mail small). Book-level; page figures not independently re-fetched.

payload.authors: Alan S. Gerber; Donald P. Green; Christopher W. Larimer
payload.title: Social Pressure and Voter Turnout: Evidence from a Large-Scale Field Experiment
payload.what_it_provides: The 'Neighbors' mailer experiment (Michigan Aug 2006 primary). Four escalating social-pressure mail treatments plus control; validated turnout outcome; the largest known direct-mail turnout effect.

payload.authors: Robert M. Bond; Christopher J. Fariss; Jason J. Jones; Adam D. I. Kramer; Cameron Marlow; Jaime E. Settle; James H. Fowler
payload.title: A 61-million-person experiment in social influence and political mobilization
payload.what_it_provides: Randomized Facebook GOTV experiment (2010 US midterms, ~61M users): direct effect of a social message vs informational vs control on validated turnout, self-expression, information-seeking, and close-friend social contagion.

supports_families: Donation / charitable giving response to an ask
payload.title: Does Price Matter in Charitable Giving? Evidence from a Large-Scale Natural Field Experiment
payload.what_it_provides: Randomized direct-mail experiment (>50,000 prior donors): presence of a matching grant, match ratio (1:1/2:1/3:1), match cap, and suggested-amount, on probability of donating and revenue per solicitation.

payload.authors: Stefano DellaVigna; John A. List; Ulrike Malmendier
supports_families: Donation / charitable giving response to an ask
payload.title: Testing for Altruism and Social Pressure in Charitable Giving
payload.what_it_provides: Door-to-door fundraising with pre-announcement flyers (opt-out option) that separates altruism from social pressure; structural estimate of the social-pressure cost of declining a solicitor.

supports_families: Political persuasion (campaign contact on vote choice)
payload.title: Persuasion: Empirical Evidence
payload.what_it_provides: Survey/framework for persuasion effects across consumers/voters/donors/investors; defines the persuasion-rate metric and reviews media-effects magnitudes (generally modest, context-dependent).

payload.authors: Craswell, N.; Zoeter, O.; Taylor, M.; Ramsey, B.
payload.title: An Experimental Comparison of Click Position-Bias Models
payload.what_it_provides: Cascade/examination click models; shows lower-rank CTR is explained by an examination process that decays down a ranked list (structural click model).

payload.authors: Joachims, T.; Granka, L.; Pan, B.; Hembrooke, H.; Gay, G.
payload.title: Accurately Interpreting Clickthrough Data as Implicit Feedback
payload.what_it_provides: Eye-tracking evidence that users scan top-down and attention drops sharply below the top 1-2 ranks; motivates clicks as position-biased implicit feedback.

payload.authors: Joachims, T.; Swaminathan, A.; Schnabel, T.
payload.title: Unbiased Learning-to-Rank with Biased Feedback
payload.what_it_provides: Position-based propensity q_r=(1/r)^eta and inverse-propensity-scoring counterfactual LTR; propensities estimated by a swap/interventional experiment. PDF read directly.

payload.title: A Spatial Model for Legislative Roll Call Analysis
payload.what_it_provides: NOMINATE spatial-voting model; low-dimensional ideal-point model predicting legislator yea/nay (basis of DW-NOMINATE).

supports_families: Veto / quorum / agenda control / stage transitions
payload.title: Political Resource Allocation, Controlled Agendas, and the Status Quo
payload.what_it_provides: Agenda-setter model: a monopoly proposer facing a reversion (status-quo) point extracts outcomes far from the median; formal comparative statics of gatekeeping/agenda control.

payload.title: Cooperation and Punishment in Public Goods Experiments
payload.what_it_provides: Public-goods game (n=4, 20 tokens, MPCR a=0.4, 10 periods, Partner/Stranger x punish/no-punish). Extracted final-period means and punishment technology (PDF read directly).

payload.title: Altruistic Punishment in Humans
payload.what_it_provides: Perfect-stranger public-goods experiment (n=4, 20 MU, 6 periods): cooperation high/sustained with costly punishment, collapses toward free-riding without.

payload.title: Crime and Punishment: An Economic Approach
payload.what_it_provides: Structural deterrence model: offenses fall in apprehension probability p and severity f; supply-of-offenses O(p,f,u) as a rational cost-benefit response.

payload.title: A Proof for the Queuing Formula: L = λW
payload.what_it_provides: Little's Law L=λW (mean number in system = arrival rate × mean sojourn); distribution-free analytic queueing identity underpinning capacity/queue models.

payload.title: A New Approach to the Economic Analysis of Nonstationary Time Series and the Business Cycle
payload.what_it_provides: Markov regime-switching model: discrete unobserved state governs the DGP; MLE + filter/smoother to infer regime probabilities and transition matrix.

payload.authors: Berg, J.; Dickhaut, J.; McCabe, K.
payload.title: Trust, Reciprocity, and Social History
payload.what_it_provides: Canonical investment/trust-game paradigm; controls for reputation, precommitment, punishment via anonymity/one-shot -> isolates reciprocity as a behavioral primitive. Provides the operational reciprocity measure (return r increasing in amount sent s).

payload.title: Fairness and Retaliation: The Economics of Reciprocity
payload.what_it_provides: Review establishing positive AND negative reciprocity as robust economic forces (people repay kindness with kindness, hostility with costly retaliation beyond self-interest).

payload.authors: Johnson, N. D.; Mislin, A. A.
payload.title: Trust games: A meta-analysis
payload.what_it_provides: Meta-analysis of 162 trust-game replications (n>23,000). Central tendencies for trust (amount sent) and trustworthiness (amount returned) and the protocol/geography moderators that shift them.

payload.title: Perceived Risk, Trust, and Democracy
payload.what_it_provides: The 'asymmetry principle': trust is created slowly but destroyed in an instant; trust-destroying (negative) events carry greater impact than trust-building (positive) events. Survey/conceptual evidence in the risk domain.

payload.title: On the Confirmability and Disconfirmability of Trait Concepts
payload.venue: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology 50(1):131-142
payload.what_it_provides: Mechanism for the asymmetry: favorable traits (like trustworthiness) require many confirming instances but few disconfirming ones to destroy; negative traits are the reverse. Grounds trust as a favorable trait that is slow to build, fast to lose.

payload.authors: Kim, P. H.; Ferrin, D. L.; Cooper, C. D.; Dirks, K. T.
payload.title: Removing the Shadow of Suspicion: The Effects of Apology Versus Denial for Repairing Competence- Versus Integrity-Based Trust Violations
payload.what_it_provides: The core violation-type x response-strategy interaction for trust repair, from two controlled experiments; the finding that competence- and integrity-based trust are DISTINCT (no single trust scalar).

payload.authors: Schweitzer, M. E.; Hershey, J. C.; Bradlow, E. T.
payload.title: Promises and Lies: Restoring Violated Trust
payload.venue: Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes 101(1):1-19
payload.what_it_provides: Repeated-trust-game evidence that trust harmed by DECEPTION never fully recovers even after apology + promise + a consistent series of trustworthy actions -> repair is bounded/partial and path-dependent on whether deception occurred.

payload.title: Evolution of indirect reciprocity by image scoring
payload.what_it_provides: The structural image-scoring model of indirect reciprocity: agents carry an image score updated by observed helping/refusing; discriminators help those with sufficient score. Analytic/simulation condition for cooperation (probability of knowing a partner's score must exceed cost/benefit ratio).

payload.authors: Resnick, P.; Zeckhauser, R.; Swanson, J.; Lockwood, K.
payload.title: The value of reputation on eBay: A controlled experiment
payload.what_it_provides: Randomized controlled FIELD experiment quantifying the causal value of online reputation: an established high-reputation identity commanded a willingness-to-pay premium over new identities. Anchors reputation as a behavior-relevant, priced signal.

payload.title: Decay functions
payload.what_it_provides: Longitudinal (4-year) banker-network study of relationship decay: relationships weaken/disappear over time; decay is SLOWER for embedded ties (many shared third parties), same-division (homophily), high-status, and strong-prior (inertia) ties, and FASTER for bridges spanning structural holes (liability of newness — young bridges decay fastest).

payload.authors: Roberts, S. G. B.; Dunbar, R. I. M.
payload.title: The costs of family and friends: an 18-month longitudinal study of relationship maintenance and decay
payload.what_it_provides: 18-month panel (N=25 students, school->university transition): emotional closeness declines when contact frequency and shared activities drop; FRIENDSHIPS decay faster than KIN ties; decline is buffered by continued investment, with a gender split (frequency of contact protects most for women, shared activities for men).
